page: Amazon Web Services r6idn.32xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features 128 vCPUs and 64 physical cores from an Intel Xeon 8375C processor operating at 3.5 GHz. The system is equipped with 1024.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core. Local storage is bundled, consisting of 7600 GB of NVMe SSD instance store volumes. Network connectivity is established with a baseline bandwidth of 200 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates strong multi-core capabilities, with the PassMark CPU score and CPU encryption tests reaching top-tier performance levels. Multi-core CPU performance via stress-ng and multi-core Geekbench scores fall into the top 25% category. Memory bandwidth tests for both small and large blocks also demonstrate strong throughput. However, single-core performance is more moderate, with stress-ng single-core and Geekbench single-core scores landing in the average tier.

From a resource density perspective, the instance offers high memory capacity and substantial local NVMe storage, making it qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads requiring large in-memory datasets and high-speed local caching. A notable tradeoff is the use of DDR4 memory rather than newer DDR5 standards, alongside average single-threaded performance. The hardware profile and benchmark results, including strong database operations and Redis SET performance, make this server appropriate for large-scale databases, in-memory caching layers, and memory-intensive data processing tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services r6idn.32xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 128 vCPUs and 64 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8375C processor running at 3.5 GHz. It includes 1024.0 GB of DDR4 memory and 7600 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, supported by a 200 Gbps baseline network speed. Benchmark results show top-tier performance in CPU encryption and overall PassMark CPU scores, alongside strong multi-core processing and memory bandwidth. However, single-core performance remains in the average tier. This resource-dense configuration provides qualitative cost efficiency for memory-heavy operations without the need for external storage overhead. It is highly suitable for large-scale databases, Redis deployments, and memory-intensive enterprise workloads.

tagline: A memory-optimized platform featuring high-capacity local NVMe storage and strong multi-core processing for demanding database workloads.

1024.0 GB of DDR4 memory and 7600 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
